seoTitle: How to Comment in Microsoft Loop (2026)
metaDescription: Add, reply to, and resolve inline comments in Microsoft Loop using keyboard shortcuts, the context menu, or table and board-level feedback.
intro: Instead of jumping to a separate chat thread every time you have feedback on a sentence, Microsoft Loop lets you leave inline comments right where the text lives. This guide covers the different ways to add comments, tag teammates, and manage feedback threads without cluttering your page.

steps.heading: Highlight the text you want to comment on
steps.body: Open your target Loop page, navigate to the relevant section, and highlight the specific text block you're giving feedback on.

steps.heading: Add a comment with a keyboard shortcut
steps.body: Press Ctrl+Alt+M on Windows or Cmd+Alt+M on Mac to instantly drop a comment box on the highlighted text.

steps.heading: Or use the context menu
steps.body: Hover over your highlighted text and click the comment bubble icon that appears in the pop-up toolbar as an alternative to the shortcut.

steps.heading: Tag teammates with @ mentions
steps.body: Type your feedback and use the @ symbol followed by a name to notify a specific team member about the comment.

steps.heading: Reply to and resolve comments
steps.body: Reply directly to existing threads, edit your own comments, or click the checkmark to resolve a comment once it's been addressed.

faq.question: Can I comment on a specific cell in a Loop table?
faq.answer: Yes, you can select a cell in a Loop table or board and click the internal comment bubble to leave a row-specific note.

faq.question: How do I notify someone about a comment?
faq.answer: Type @ followed by their name inside your comment field to tag them and trigger a notification.

faq.question: Can I see all open comments on a page in one place?
faq.answer: You can use the activity pane to review comment threads across a page rather than hunting through the document manually.

faq.question: What happens when I resolve a comment?
faq.answer: Resolving a comment marks it as addressed and typically collapses or archives the thread, keeping the page cleaner while preserving the history.
